# Readme Quality Checklist: Beautiful README

**Purpose**: Validate the quality, clarity, and completeness of the requirements
documented in `spec.md`, `plan.md`, `research.md`, `data-model.md`,
`contracts/`, and `quickstart.md` for the Beautiful README feature. These
are "unit tests for English" — they check whether the requirements are
written well enough to be implemented without follow-up questions, NOT
whether the implementation works.

**Created**: 2026-06-05

**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are visual-hierarchy requirements (hero above-the-fold) quantified with a specific viewport size in pixels AND a specific theme? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001, §SC-002]
- [ ] CHK002 Are badge-variant decisions (default vs. secondary vs. outline vs. ghost) specified per slot with a stated rationale? [Completeness, Spec §FR-002, Gap]
- [ ] CHK003 Are badge-size decisions (xs / sm / default / lg) specified per row (status row vs. editor-support row vs. plugin-block row)? [Completeness, Spec §FR-002, Gap]
- [ ] CHK004 Are accessibility requirements (WCAG AA) specified per surface — light theme AND dark theme — with the metric (contrast ratio, target ≥ 4.5:1)? [Completeness, Spec §FR-006, Gap]
- [ ] CHK005 Is an alt-text style guide specified for every badge image so screen-reader and npm-stripped views convey the same information? [Completeness, Spec §FR-006, Gap]
- [ ] CHK006 Are plugin-block template requirements (number of feature bullets, code-block structure, deep-link anchor format) explicitly enumerated in the data model? [Completeness, Data Model §Entity 3, Gap]
- [ ] CHK007 Are content-preservation requirements enumerated as a list of sections that MUST remain verbatim or with limited edits? [Completeness, Spec §FR-009, Gap]
- [ ] CHK008 Is the "live data" requirement for each status badge specific about which data source (npm registry endpoint, GitHub Actions workflow file, GitHub Releases API) feeds which badge? [Completeness, Spec §FR-005, US3]
- [ ] CHK009 Is the relationship between shieldcn.dev badge URLs and their underlying data sources documented? (Shieldcn proxies live data — is the proxy contract stated?) [Completeness, Spec §FR-002 + FR-005, Gap]
- [ ] CHK010 Are the editor-support badge colors / variants specified for each of the three editors (Claude Code, Cursor, OpenCode)? [Completeness, Spec §FR-004, Gap]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK011 Is "above the fold" defined with a specific viewport size and a specific theme (light + dark)? [Clarity, Spec §US1 + §SC-002]
- [ ] CHK012 Is "within 5 seconds" (US1 acceptance) defined with a measurable test protocol (manual review count, Lighthouse metric, etc.)? [Measurability, Spec §US1, §SC-001]
- [ ] CHK013 Is the CI badge "live data" requirement explicit about which GitHub Actions workflow file and which branch is monitored? [Clarity, Spec §US3, §FR-005]
- [ ] CHK014 Is the 30 KB size budget measured in bytes (uncompressed UTF-8), with the measuring tool specified (`wc -c`)? [Clarity, Spec §FR-007]
- [ ] CHK015 Is the 250 KB `marketplace.html` budget the same budget from the constitution Principle IV, or a new figure? (Cross-reference) [Clarity, Spec §SC-004 + Constitution §IV]
- [ ] CHK016 Is "polished" or "visual identity" defined with objective, measurable criteria (badge density per row, whitespace, font weights, color tokens)? [Ambiguity, Spec §US1]
- [ ] CHK017 Is the term "fallback" (for shieldcn.dev outage) defined as automatic switch, manual swap, or maintainer-triggered? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-002, Edge Case]
- [ ] CHK018 Is "primary visual badges" distinguished from "documented fallbacks" in a way the README author can act on without judgement calls? [Clarity, Spec §FR-002]
- [ ] CHK019 Is the SC-007 "qualitative adoption guard-rail" (views / clone ratio) defined with a measurement window (e.g., 14 days before vs. 14 days after) and a threshold for "no regression"? [Clarity, Spec §SC-007, Gap]
- [ ] CHK020 Is "hand-edited README" (the assumption in `spec.md` Assumptions) documented as a build-step exclusion so future contributors know not to introduce a renderer? [Clarity, Spec §Assumptions, Gap]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK021 Do US1 (hero above the fold), FR-001 (hero above the fold), and SC-002 (hero above the fold) all reference the same viewport size and theme set? [Consistency, Spec §US1, §FR-001, §SC-002]
- [ ] CHK022 Do FR-002 (shieldcn primary + shields.io fallback) and FR-005 (live data badges) align on which badge slots are static vs. live? [Consistency, Spec §FR-002 + §FR-005]
- [ ] CHK023 Do the spec's plugin-list ("typescript-rules" and "jsdoc-standards") match the actual `marketplace.json` and the per-plugin README paths? [Consistency, Spec §US2 + `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json`]
- [ ] CHK024 Does the data-model entity "Plugin Block" match the README's per-plugin block template (T011/T012)? [Consistency, Data Model §Entity 3 + `tasks.md` §Phase 4]
- [ ] CHK025 Do the contracts in `contracts/badge-url-schemas.md` reference the same `package.json#name` (`devkit-ai`) and the same repo URL (`pau-vega/ai-devkit`) as the README and the spec? [Consistency, Contracts + Spec §FR-002]
- [ ] CHK026 Do the "no new runtime dependencies" assumption (Spec §Assumptions, FR-008) and the constitution's Principle I dependency-pinning rule agree on what counts as a dependency? [Consistency, Spec §Assumptions + Constitution §I]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK027 Can SC-001 ("visitor identifies project name, editors, install command within 5 seconds") be objectively measured by a third party, or is it subjective? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001]
- [ ] CHK028 Can SC-002 ("hero is fully visible above the fold on 1280×800 in light and dark") be objectively verified by an automated screenshot diff? [Measurability, Spec §SC-002]
- [ ] CHK029 Can SC-003 ("100% of badge URLs return HTTP 200 in CI") be objectively verified by the proposed link-check job? [Measurability, Spec §SC-003]
- [ ] CHK030 Can SC-004 ("README < 30 KB and marketplace.html < 250 KB") be objectively verified by `wc -c` + the existing build script? [Measurability, Spec §SC-004]
- [ ] CHK031 Can SC-006 ("every shipped plugin renders in the same visual template") be objectively verified by a visual diff or a structural assertion (e.g., "all plugin blocks have the same set of badge URLs")? [Measurability, Spec §SC-006]
- [ ] CHK032 Is the SC-007 measurement window specified in days AND is a "regression" threshold defined (e.g., "no more than 10% drop")? [Measurability, Spec §SC-007, Gap]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK033 Are the three user stories' interaction paths (P1 hero scan, P2 catalog browse, P3 status check) non-overlapping and individually testable? [Coverage, Spec §US1-3]
- [ ] CHK034 Are requirements specified for the npm-registry page rendering (text-only fallback view)? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, FR-006]
- [ ] CHK035 Are requirements specified for mobile / narrow-viewport (< 400 px) rendering of the badge rows and tables? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, Gap]
- [ ] CHK036 Are requirements specified for the "new plugin" onboarding path — what does a contributor do to add a third plugin to the catalog? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, Gap]
- [ ] CHK037 Are requirements specified for the "GitHub username / repo rename" path so badge URLs don't drift silently? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, Gap]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [ ] CHK038 Are requirements specified for when `shieldcn.dev` is unreachable for an extended period (multi-day outage)? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-002, Gap]
- [ ] CHK039 Are requirements specified for when a badge URL returns 5xx in CI — does the link-check job block the PR, or warn? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-005 + §FR-010, Gap]
- [ ] CHK040 Are requirements specified for when the GitHub repo URL case differs (`pau-vega/ai-devkit` vs. `pau-vega/AI-Devkit`)? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, Gap]
- [ ] CHK041 Are requirements specified for when a plugin description is very long and overflows the badge row layout? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, Gap]
- [ ] CHK042 Are requirements specified for when an editor is added or removed from the supported set — does the plugin-block template automatically reflect the change? [Edge Case, Spec §US2, Gap]
- [ ] CHK043 Are requirements specified for when a new badge type is requested that does not exist on shieldcn.dev — is the fallback path documented? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-002, Gap]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [ ] CHK044 Are performance requirements (size budget, render time) specified for both the README and the embedded excerpts in `marketplace.html`? [Coverage, Spec §FR-007, §SC-004]
- [ ] CHK045 Are accessibility requirements (WCAG AA, color contrast, alt text, keyboard focus) specified for every interactive element in the rendered README? [Coverage, Spec §FR-006, Gap]
- [ ] CHK046 Are security requirements (no JavaScript, no vendored assets, no third-party widgets, no inline event handlers) specified and traced to a threat model? [Coverage, Spec §FR-008, Gap]
- [ ] CHK047 Are compatibility requirements (GitHub web light + dark, npm registry text view, `marketplace.html` excerpts) specified as testable targets? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, Gap]
- [ ] CHK048 Are observability requirements (CI link-check, CI markdownlint, CI byte-size) specified as release gates, not as follow-up suggestions? [Coverage, Spec §FR-010, Gap]
- [ ] CHK049 Are maintainability requirements specified — what does the README look like 6 months from now, and what is the cost of keeping it in sync with the plugin list? [Coverage, Spec §Assumptions, Gap]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK050 Is the assumption that "no new runtime dependencies" is required validated by the constitution (Principle I) and traced to a specific rule? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions + Constitution §I]
- [ ] CHK051 Is the assumption that "per-plugin READMEs are out of scope" stated as a hard boundary, with a follow-up tracking issue referenced? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions, Gap]
- [ ] CHK052 Is the assumption that "shadcn tokens meet WCAG AA out of the box" validated with a source link (shadcn/ui theming guide) and a contrast measurement? [Assumption, Spec §research.md §7, Gap]
- [ ] CHK053 Is the assumption that "GitHub username is case-insensitive" documented with the redirect target so a badge URL never breaks silently? [Assumption, Spec §research.md §10, Gap]
- [ ] CHK054 Is the dependency on `shieldcn.dev` (a third-party service) explicitly listed as an external dependency with a documented outage fallback? [Dependency, Spec §FR-002, Gap]
- [ ] CHK055 Is the dependency on GitHub Actions / npm registry / GitHub Releases as live data sources documented with their availability SLAs? [Dependency, Spec §FR-005, Gap]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [ ] CHK056 Is "polished" or "visual identity" defined with measurable criteria, or is it left to the implementer's taste? [Ambiguity, Spec §US1]
- [ ] CHK057 Is the term "lightweight" (in `spec.md` Assumptions: "no vendored fonts") defined with a byte-size threshold for "vendored"? [Ambiguity, Spec §Assumptions, Gap]
- [ ] CHK058 Is the screenshot-diff check (mentioned in `plan.md` and `quickstart.md` step 4–5) classified as a CI gate, a follow-up, or a manual-only step? [Ambiguity, Spec §plan.md, §quickstart.md, Gap]
- [ ] CHK059 Is the relationship between the "hero" (US1) and the "Table of Contents" (T004) defined — is the TOC part of the hero, immediately after the hero, or floating? [Ambiguity, Spec §US1 + Data Model §Entity 4, Gap]
- [ ] CHK060 Is the editor-support badge row placement specified — is it in the hero (US1) or repeated inside each plugin block (US2)? [Ambiguity, Spec §US1 + §US2, Gap]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Link to relevant resources or documentation
- Items are numbered sequentially for easy reference
- This checklist is **additive** to `specs/001-beautiful-readme/checklists/requirements.md` (the spec-quality checklist created by `/speckit.specify`); it focuses on the "unit tests for English" angle, while `requirements.md` focuses on the spec's internal completeness.
